description: Formation mechanism inference from junction forensics (cheat29), SD substrate analysis (cheat14 + cheat27), and TE enrichment (cheat21 + cheat28). Outputs: mechanism class (NAHR/NHEJ/MMEJ), microhomology length at junction, flanking SD characteristics, TE content. Group-independent — junction sequence doesn't need carrier labels.
question: Q4

mechanism_class.description: NAHR = flanking SDs >1kb at >95% identity; NHEJ = no microhomology; MMEJ = 2-25bp microhomology at junction; TE_mediated = flanking TEs of same family in inverted orientation

microhomology_length_bp.description: Length of sequence identity at the junction. 0 = blunt, 1-4 = NHEJ with noise, 2-25 = MMEJ signature, >25 = NAHR

sd_identity_left.description: % sequence identity of left flanking SD

sd_concordance.description: cheat27 output: do minimap2 and RepeatMasker agree that flanking SDs support NAHR?

te_families.description: TE families found at both breakpoints (suggests TE-mediated if concordant)

tandem_repeat_context.description: cheat28 output: tandem repeat density ±5kb of each junction

mechanism_confidence.description: high = both junctions agree + independent SD/TE evidence; medium = one junction resolved; low = ambiguous
